_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Integrate new systems into existing network architecture.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of networking fundamentals relevant to data systems, including TCP/IP, DNS resolution, HTTP/HTTPS protocols, firewall and security group configurations, load balancing, and how network architecture affects data pipeline design and performance.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-K-013 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AI-T-015 (AI, cosine: 0.401), DA-T-001 (DA, cosine: 0.373).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.8/10 (raw 0.481). Shared vocabulary: [architecture, network, systems]. DCWF-only terms [existing, integrate, new] vs WIDAI-only terms [affects, balancing, configurations, data, design, dns] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.864 (P=0.892, R=0.838). P exceeds R by 0.054 — the DCWF element's 7 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 31 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.4%). "Integrate new systems into existing network architecture." and "Knowledge of networking fundamentals relevant to data systems, including TCP/IP," are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.415 < STS 0.481 (gap -0.067). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [existing, integrate, new] and [affects, balancing, configurations] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 8.8%. Shared tokens: [architecture, network, systems]. DCWF-only: [existing, integrate, new]. WIDAI-only: [affects, balancing, configurations, data, design, dns].
